title: Programming Foundations
description: Start here if you're new to programming. Builds from storing data, to organizing logic into reusable functions, to handling things going wrong gracefully.

title: Data Structures & Algorithms Core
description: Covers the fundamental linear data structures and the vocabulary (Big O) used to compare algorithm efficiency — prerequisite for later DSA topics like trees and graphs.

title: Database Fundamentals
description: From designing a well-structured schema, to querying across related tables, to making those queries fast at scale.

title: Networking Essentials
description: The conceptual model first, then addressing, then the protocol you'll interact with most as a web developer.

title: Operating Systems Core
description: Concurrency fundamentals, the classic failure mode (deadlock) that comes with it, and how the OS gives every process its own memory illusion.

title: Software Engineering Practice
description: How real teams plan work, track changes safely, and verify their code actually works before shipping it.

title: Security Fundamentals
description: The core distinctions and practices every developer needs before building anything that handles user data.

title: Intro to AI & Machine Learning
description: From the core ML paradigms, to how neural networks actually learn, to working effectively with modern LLM-based tools like COSIB itself.

title: Mathematics for Computing Primer
description: The discrete math building blocks that show up repeatedly across programming, databases, and AI.
